sanskrit:
यथाकाशस्थितो नित्यं वायुः सर्वत्रगो महान्।
तथा सर्वाणि भूतानि मत्स्थानीत्युपधारय।।
transliteration:
yathākāśa-sthito nityaṃ vāyuḥ sarvatra-go mahān
tathā sarvāṇi bhūtāni mat-sthānīty upadhāraya
literal_meaning: Just as the mighty wind, moving everywhere, always rests in space, know that in the same way all beings rest in Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna offers Arjuna a cognitive reframe: the universe is not chaotic but held within a boundless, non-intrusive container—an invitation to shift from anxious hyper-vigilance to secure base.
psychological_state: Moving from anxious over-control to trusting containment; a felt sense that one can exhale because existence itself is safe.
trigger_scenario: A client who micromanages every detail of life finally experiences a moment of letting go while watching clouds drift across an open sky.
anchor_text: Rest like the wind in space, held securely within the vastness of awareness.
